narrative: Today's fire-theme papers trace human fingerprints on disturbance regimes, from a late-19th-century industrial pulse in Appalachian fire activity to fire-adapted serotiny in a Yunnan pine and pan-boreal forest-age mapping shaped by wildfire and logging history. Climate coverage centres on background tree mortality rising across eastern North America under warming, canopy buffering of temperature effects on oak and beech regeneration along elevation gradients, and a new global isoscape of atmospheric CO2 for tracing biosphere-atmosphere exchange. Movement and population work is strong: three-dimensional step-selection models extend animal movement analysis into swimming and flying species, jaguar demography in Amazonian floodplains reveals a concerning shift toward male-biased populations, and a global assessment maps seasonal variation in avian diets. Community and landscape studies span reintroduced digging mammals reshaping insect assemblages, ant-mediated seed dispersal responding to landscape configuration in tropical agricultural mosaics, and genetic diversity concentrated in ancient grasslands. Conservation highlights include a systematic review showing floodplain restoration boosts biodiversity despite invasive-species setbacks, three decades of IUCN Red List assessments charting shark and ray extinction risk, and a five-year evaluation of structured expert elicitation in adaptive management. On the methods and biogeography front, a new framework integrates eDNA, graph theory and niche modelling for ecosystem assessment, while evolutionary papers examine topographic gene-flow barriers in a vector insect and the deep phylogenomic origins of streptophyte multicellularity.

methods_radar.title: Comparative species distribution model framework for marine conservation and its application to loggerhead turtles in the Mediterranean
methods_radar.note: Develops a new comparative species distribution model benchmarking framework for marine conservation planning.

methods_radar.title: A New Framework to Empower Ecosystem Assessment Through the Integration of eDNA Inventories, Graph Theory and Niche Modelling
methods_radar.note: Introduces a new framework combining eDNA, graphlet network analysis and niche modelling.

methods_radar.title: Defaunation Maps for Tropical Mammals and Birds
methods_radar.note: Develops novel quantitative modelling frameworks linking hunting pressure to pantropical defaunation hotspot maps.

methods_radar.title: Modelling density surfaces from integrated passive acoustic and point count data streams
methods_radar.note: Presents a novel model integrating passive acoustic monitoring with point-count data for absolute density estimation.

methods_radar.title: BIMr2: Inference of migration rates and their drivers from multilocus genotypes
methods_radar.note: Introduces BIMr2, a new tool for inferring migration rates and environmental drivers from multilocus genotypes.

methods_radar.title: ForestForTrees: An R package to infer forest structure from remote sensing data
methods_radar.note: Introduces ForestForTrees, an R package inferring forest size-abundance structure from remote sensing without advanced Bayesian expertise.

methods_radar.title: sampley: A Python package for sampling visual survey data
methods_radar.note: Introduces sampley, a Python package standardizing spatiotemporal sampling of visual survey data.

methods_radar.title: From video to behaviour: An LSTM‐based approach for automated nest behaviour recognition in the wild
methods_radar.note: Presents a deployment-focused LSTM deep-learning framework for automated nest behaviour recognition in the wild.

methods_radar.title: Highly fragmented European wetlands with uneven restoration needs
methods_radar.note: Uses satellite imagery and machine learning to newly map fragmentation across six European wetland types.

methods_radar.title: Alpine treeline ecotones world wide: fine‐scale spatial patterns and environmental controls
methods_radar.note: Uses very-high-resolution satellite imagery and landscape metrics to newly characterize global treeline ecotone patterns.

methods_radar.title: Estimation of Forest Soil Organic Carbon Density in Dry Tropical Valleys Based on Multi-source Data and Machine Learning Algorithm
methods_radar.note: Combines multi-source data with machine learning to estimate forest soil organic carbon density.

methods_radar.title: Multiscale species distribution models reveal seascape niche partitioning and overlap in native and alien fishes
methods_radar.note: Extends species distribution models to multiscale seascape niche partitioning for native/alien fishes.

methods_radar.title: A multi-satellite comparison of forest growth between karst and adjacent non-karst nature reserves
methods_radar.note: Applies multi-satellite comparison approach to benchmark forest growth across karst vs non-karst reserves.

methods_radar.title: Addressing false negatives and positives in eDNA studies
methods_radar.note: Evaluates and addresses false positive/negative detection error reporting standards in eDNA studies.

methods_radar.title: Cell‐based crop phenotyping for future climates
methods_radar.note: Advances fluorescence-imaging crop phenotyping throughput using robotics and machine learning.

methods_radar.title: Changing patterns of colonisation and persistence during the wolf recolonisation of the human‐dominated Italian alpine region
methods_radar.note: Applies dynamic occupancy models to track wolf recolonisation patterns in the Italian Alps.

methods_radar.title: Species traits mediate the abundant‐center patterns in ground‐dwelling mammal and bird species in China
methods_radar.note: Uses standardized camera-trap abundance indices to test the abundant-center hypothesis across China.

methods_radar.title: Ground‐to‐canopy monitoring reveals hidden ecological patterns in Congo Basin mammals
methods_radar.note: Deploys ground-to-canopy camera trapping to reveal vertically stratified mammal community patterns in the Congo Basin.

methods_radar.title: Spectrally Guided Delineation of Structurally Homogeneous Vegetation Patches Using Sentinel-2 Imagery in Heterogeneous Post-Industrial Landscapes
methods_radar.note: Applies Sentinel-2 spectral imagery to delineate vegetation patches in post-industrial landscapes.

methods_radar.title: Predicting Coral Offspring Survival From Reef Thermal Histories in Ningaloo World Heritage Reef
methods_radar.note: Uses boosted regression trees on satellite SST data to predict coral larval survival from thermal history.

methods_radar.title: eDNA sampling reveals no evidence of negative effects of beaver recolonisation on the catchment‐scale distribution of migratory fish
methods_radar.note: Applies catchment-scale eDNA sampling to assess beaver-fish co-distribution impacts.

methods_radar.title: Effects of urban vegetation on the surface urban heat island: a UAV approach with a focus on private gardens
methods_radar.note: Applies UAV imagery to study urban heat island effects on private gardens; not a new method itself.

methods_radar.title: Resolving Emergent Patterns in Community Genetics With Environmental DNA
methods_radar.note: Applies environmental DNA approaches to resolve community genetics patterns across space and time.

methods_radar.title: Asymmetric transferability of trait–environment relationships between native and exotic plant species in Singaporean forests differing in land‐use history
methods_radar.note: Applies joint species distribution modelling to test trait-environment transferability across systems.
